dashboard_mp_connections_no_cars: No vehicle declarations on file for this parliament yet
mp_cars_page_description: Every passenger car or jeep declared by an MP (or their spouse) in the most recent declaration on file, sorted by declared value in euros.
mp_cars_page_footer: Cars (passenger vehicles + jeeps) extracted from the most-recent declaration of every MP. Spouse-held cars are listed with holder = spouse. Source: register.cacbg.bg (Bulgarian Court of Audit).
mp_assets_source_note: Combined declarant and spouse holdings; source: Bulgarian Court of Audit (Сметна палата).
officials_assets_page_description: Bulgarian cabinet members, state-agency heads, and regional governors ranked by net worth from their most recent property/interest declaration with the Court of Audit.
officials_assets_page_footer: Net worth = sum of declared real estate, vehicles, cash, bank deposits, receivables, investments, securities and company shares (declarant + spouse) minus declared debts. Source: register.cacbg.bg (Bulgarian Court of Audit). Scope: cabinet, state-agency heads, and regional governors. Mayors and judiciary are tracked separately.
official_not_found_desc: No declarations on file under this slug. The official may be from a category we don't ingest, or the URL is wrong.
official_connections_note: Companies this official is tied to via declared stakes or the Commerce Registry, and the MPs and officials who share those companies. Name-only Commerce Registry matches are not corroborated.
official_source_note: Source: register.cacbg.bg (Bulgarian Court of Audit). Asset values declared by the official.
official_assets_unvalued_footnote: {{count}} real-estate item(s) declared without a value.
mp_assets_page_description: Bulgarian MPs ranked by net worth from their most recent property/interest declaration filed with the Court of Audit.
mp_assets_page_footer: Net worth = sum of declared real estate, vehicles, cash, bank deposits, receivables, investments, securities and company shares (declarant + spouse) minus declared debts. Source: register.cacbg.bg (Bulgarian Court of Audit). Each MP's most recent filed declaration is used.
mp_assets_no_data: No declared assets data for this candidate.
connections_edge_inferred: Inferred from a name match — not a corroborated record
mp_connections_full_intro: Each row is a shortest path through the business connections graph from this MP to another. Direct shared companies (2 steps) usually indicate an explicit business overlap; longer paths show indirect links through co-officers or owners.
mp_connections_no_paths: No paths to other MPs in the data
mp_connections_no_paths_long: No business-connection paths to other MPs were found for this candidate. Either they have no declared companies, or none of those companies share an officer/owner with another MP.
mp_connections_neighborhood_hint: Showing immediate 1-hop business neighborhood (no MP→MP paths found).
mp_connections_path_graph_hint: Path subgraph — click a node for details. Ctrl/Cmd + scroll to zoom, drag to pan.
mp_voting_source_note: Loyalty = share of cast votes that matched the party group majority. Absences excluded.
mp_voting_full_intro: Loyalty is the share of votes the MP cast in line with their party group's majority. Absences are excluded.
mp_voting_none_long: No roll-call voting record was found for this candidate. They may not be currently seated, or the parliament they served in has not yet been ingested.
mp_voting_per_session_pending: Per-vote breakdown and dissent deep-links arrive in the next phase, alongside the session pages.
votes_index_description: Every voting day in the Bulgarian National Assembly, with per-MP and per-party breakdowns.
votes_index_intro: Every voting day in the National Assembly. Click a date to see how MPs voted on each item.
votes_index_empty: No roll-call sessions have been ingested yet.
votes_session_not_found: No roll-call data was found for this date.
votes_session_no_party_data: Per-party breakdown unavailable for this item.
cohesion_outlier_title: Most independent and most loyal MPs
mp_similarity_empty: Not enough vote overlap to compute peers.
votes_landing_breaks_empty: No contested votes in the last 7 days.
votes_landing_pair_intro: Items where {{partyA}} and {{partyB}} voted opposite ways
votes_landing_pair_empty: No breaks found between these groups in the current parliament.
votes_heatmap_axis_groups: Parliamentary groups · items sorted by closeness · each cell shows the group's vote split
votes_session_turnout_hint: Share of seated MPs who cast a vote (yes/no/abstain), averaged across items.
votes_session_cohesion_hint: Average share of each group voting the same way, weighted by group size. 100% = every group unanimous.
votes_session_dissents_hint: Number of (MP × item) votes against the MP's own group plurality.
votes_session_closest_hint: Smallest |yes − (no + abstain)| margin among the session's items.
votes_session_dissents_lead: MPs who broke with the group plurality.
cohesion_description: How unified Bulgarian parliamentary groups vote — mean and median cohesion per item across the current parliament.
cohesion_intro: How unified each parliamentary group is when its members vote. Cohesion = share of the group voting the same way per item, averaged across every item the group participated in. 1.00 means every member voted the same way every time; 0.50 is an even split. Absences are excluded.
cohesion_empty: No cohesion data has been computed yet — run the derived-metrics step first.
cohesion_methodology_note: The list aggregates cohesion across every item ingested; the chart shows per-session mean cohesion. Absences are excluded from both.
party_cohesion_hint: Share of group members voting the same way per item, averaged. Absences excluded.
cohesion_chart_y_hint: Y axis: per-session mean cohesion (1.0 = unanimous). Lines connect dates the group voted on at least one item.
similarity_description: Cosine similarity between Bulgarian MPs over their roll-call vote vectors — top peers within and across parliamentary groups.
similarity_intro: Pick an MP to see whose votes most closely match theirs. Cross-party twins — MPs from different parliamentary groups who consistently vote the same way — appear first. Similarity is cosine similarity over yes/no vote vectors; absences are excluded.
similarity_pick_prompt: Choose an MP above to see their voting twins.
similarity_no_data: No similarity data is available for this MP yet.
similarity_cross_party_hint: MPs from other parliamentary groups whose votes most closely match this MP. These signal informal blocs that cross party lines.
similarity_same_party_hint: Top peers within this MP's own parliamentary group, ranked by vote similarity.
embedding_description: 2D UMAP projection of every sitting Bulgarian MP's roll-call vote vector — clusters reveal informal blocs.
embedding_intro: Each dot is one MP. Distance approximates how differently two MPs vote — neighbours vote the same way most of the time. Layout is a UMAP projection of the full vote-vector space, coloured by parliamentary group. Clusters reveal informal blocs.
embedding_empty: No embedding data has been computed yet — run the derived-metrics step first.
embedding_outliers_hint: MPs whose nearest neighbours are mostly from a different parliamentary group — the most plausible candidates for cross-party voting.
embedding_outliers_empty: Every MP's nearest neighbours stay within their own group.
embedding_interaction_hint: Drag to pan · scroll to zoom · or use the controls
mp_twins_intro: Other MPs whose voting record most closely matches this one. Cross-party twins — MPs from a different parliamentary group — appear first.
hub_description: Roll-call voting analytics for the Bulgarian National Assembly: archive, group cohesion, voting twins, and a 2D map of MP voting behaviour.
hub_intro: Roll-call voting data from the Bulgarian National Assembly. Each tile previews one analysis — click into a tile for the full breakdown.
hub_similarity_lede: Largest cross-party overlap: the MP whose voting record best matches MPs from other groups.
hub_cohesion_lede: Average within-group unanimity per parliamentary group. 100% means every member always voted together.
hub_data_note: Source: parliament.bg stenograms. Per-MP, per-item votes are extracted from the official roll-call CSV attached to each plenary day.
hub_votes_desc: Every plenary day with a roll-call vote. Drill into items, tallies, and per-party breakdowns.
hub_cohesion_desc: How unified each parliamentary group votes. Mean, median, and trend over time.
hub_similarity_desc: Cosine similarity between MPs. Cross-party twins surfaced first — the informal blocs that cross party lines.
hub_embedding_desc: 2D UMAP projection of every MP's vote vector. Clusters reveal blocs; bridge MPs reveal cross-party voters.
dashboard_parliament_voting_desc: How parliamentary groups vote in relation to one another. Cells show cosine similarity of each pair's majority-vote vectors — greener cells mean the two groups vote alike, redder cells mean they vote against each other.
dashboard_parliament_legend_note: Values are % cosine over per-item majority vectors.
connections_no_pairs: No MP↔MP connections match the current filters.
connections_find_intro: Pick any two MPs — we'll find the shortest path between them through declared companies and shared associates.
connections_find_no_path: No path was found between these two MPs through declared companies or shared associates.
connections_find_empty: Pick a starting MP above to begin.
connections_hero_matrix_caption: Party × party MP↔MP ties — click a cell to drill in.
tr_no_current_records: No currently-active officers or owners on file.
tr_source_commerce_registry: Source: Bulgarian Commerce Registry (Търговски регистър) open data on data.egov.bg. Roles include both currently held and historical ones, since active management while seated is restricted by ЗПК Art. 35.
dashboard_governments_hint: Each cabinet since 2005 shown as a coloured band, with annual GDP growth, inflation, and unemployment overlaid. Click "see details" for the full page including governance indices, trust trends, EU funds and OSCE/ODIHR observation reports.
governments_description: Every Bulgarian cabinet since 2005, set against the macroeconomic backdrop of its term.
governments_explainer: Each cabinet's tenure is marked with a coloured band, overlaid with Eurostat data for annual GDP growth, inflation and unemployment. Dashed vertical lines mark parliamentary elections.
governments_chart_governance: Governance: corruption perception and Worldwide Governance Indicators
governments_chart_governance_explainer: Transparency International's CPI scores Bulgaria 0–100 (higher = cleaner). World Bank WGI scores run roughly −2.5 to +2.5 (higher = better). They are on different scales, so toggle them one at a time to read the trend.
governments_chart_cpi_explainer: Annual CPI score for Bulgaria on a 0–100 scale (0 = highly corrupt, 100 = very clean). The Y-axis is zoomed to 30–60 because the score has hovered in the low 40s for the entire period.
governments_chart_wgi_explainer: Three of the six WGI dimensions, all on a roughly −2.5 to +2.5 scale (higher = better). The Y-axis is zoomed to −0.5 to +0.7 to make Bulgaria's movement legible.
governments_chart_trust_explainer: Standard Eurobarometer "tend to trust" share for Bulgarian respondents — annual mean of the spring and autumn waves. Trust in the EU has consistently been higher than in domestic institutions.
governments_chart_fiscal: Fiscal: debt, budget balance, current account
governments_chart_fiscal_explainer: Quarterly Eurostat series, all expressed as % of GDP. Government debt (Maastricht definition) tracks the stock; the budget balance is net lending/borrowing of general government per quarter (negative = deficit); the current account is the external balance with the rest of the world. Quarterly figures swing more than the annual averages each cabinet is judged by.
governments_chart_fiscal_nominal_explain: Same fiscal series in nominal euros rather than as a share of GDP — what each cabinet adds in absolute terms. The stock chart shows the gross debt level at the end of every quarter; the flows chart shows net new debt issued per quarter (the quarter-on-quarter change in the debt stock), the budget balance (negative = deficit financed by new borrowing), and the current account balance with the rest of the world.
governments_chart_fiscal_nominal_flows: Quarterly flows — new debt issued, budget balance, current account
governments_chart_fiscal_reserve_explain: End-of-quarter stock of cash held by the state, in EUR. Not to be confused with the current account above (an external-trade flow that can be any sign) or with BNB's foreign-exchange reserves (which back the currency board). The Law on Public Finance requires the fiscal reserve to stay above a floor set in each year's State Budget Law — currently 4.5 BGN bn ≈ €2.3 bn (dashed line). Sourced from the Ministry of Finance monthly КФП bulletin.
governments_chart_government_size: Government revenue, expenditure and inward investment (EUR)
governments_chart_government_size_explai: Quarterly general government revenue and expenditure (seasonally and calendar adjusted) — the vertical gap between them equals the quarterly deficit. Below, two annual context tiles: nominal GDP (Eurostat current prices) and net foreign direct investment received by Bulgaria (BPM6, available from 2013).
debt_emissions_explainer: Per-issuance detail: every meaningful Bulgarian sovereign debt instrument, both international (Eurobonds since 2002) and domestic (BNB auctions of treasury bonds / ДЦК, 2019 onward). For each: principal, coupon rate, weighted-average settlement yield, term and maturity. International entries are hand-curated from Ministry of Finance press releases and Luxembourg Stock Exchange listings. Domestic entries are scraped from the BNB Fiscal Agent auction archive — each row is one auction event (reopenings of the same emission appear as separate rows). Bids-rejected auctions show 0 principal sold and are flagged in notes. ISINs are shown for Eurobonds where verifiable.
governments_chart_inflation_breakdown: Inflation breakdown: food, energy, services, core
governments_chart_inflation_breakdown_ex: Quarterly mean of the monthly HICP year-on-year rates by ECOICOP group. Stacking is illustrative — each component is a separate YoY rate, not weighted by basket share — but it makes the dominant driver of any given period visually obvious (e.g. the energy spike in 2022).
governments_chart_sentiment_explainer: Quarterly means of the monthly EU business and consumer surveys. Consumer confidence is a balance (positive = more optimistic than pessimistic); the Economic Sentiment Indicator is an index where 100 equals the long-term average. Sentiment usually leads the macro turning points by a quarter or two.
governments_chart_social_explainer: Four indicators of how the macro environment lands on households: youth unemployment, year-on-year change in the house price index, the Gini coefficient of disposable income (higher = more unequal), and the at-risk-of-poverty rate (% of population below 60% of median income). House prices and youth unemployment are quarterly; Gini and poverty are annual with ~1-year publication lag.
governments_chart_eu_funds_explainer: Annual EU budget flows for Bulgaria, sourced directly from the European Commission's per-Member-State budget data (TOTAL EXPENDITURE and TOTAL National contributions rows in the BG column). The blue line is total EU spending in Bulgaria (cohesion + CAP + recovery instruments); the orange line is Bulgaria's contribution to the EU budget (VAT, GNI, plastic-packaging-waste own resources). The visual gap between the two equals Bulgaria's net annual benefit. The 2008 marker flags the EU's mid-year freeze of ~€825M in pre-accession funds (PHARE/ISPA/SAPARD) and the permanent loss of ~€220M of PHARE after OLAF investigations — though gross 2008 disbursements still rose YoY because CAP direct payments and pre-financing kept flowing.
governments_observations_explainer: Each parliamentary election since 2005 has been observed by OSCE/ODIHR. The mission reports below summarise the international assessment of campaign conduct, voter-list integrity, and recurring concerns such as vote buying.
observations_description: International election observation mission reports for every Bulgarian parliamentary election since 2005 — findings, recommendations and links to full OSCE/ODIHR reports.
observations_explainer: Every parliamentary election held in Bulgaria since 2005 has been observed by OSCE/ODIHR. The mission reports assess campaign conduct, voter-list integrity, media coverage, campaign-finance transparency, and recurring concerns such as vote buying. Summaries below are generated by Claude AI; click Open report for the full ODIHR publication.
gov_pm_prior_party: Prime Minister's prior party (caretaker, formally non-partisan)
governments_headline_tile_explainer: GDP growth, HICP inflation and unemployment over each cabinet's term. For the full set of fiscal, governance and social indicators, see the indicators page.
indicators_page_description: Bulgaria's macroeconomic, fiscal, governance and social indicators since 2005, set against each cabinet's term.
indicators_page_explainer: Each cabinet's tenure is shown as a coloured band so trends can be read against who was in power. Sources are Eurostat, World Bank Worldwide Governance Indicators, Transparency International CPI and Standard Eurobarometer.
indicators_compare_toggle_on_tooltip: Turn on peer overlay — adds RO, GR, HU and HR lines to every chart on this page (EU27 is always shown as a dashed reference).
indicators_compare_toggle_off_tooltip: Turn off the four peer-country lines. EU27 reference stays.
indicators_economy_description: Bulgaria's growth, inflation and labour-market indicators since 2005, set against each cabinet's term.
indicators_fiscal_description: Public-finance indicators: debt, balance, reserve, government size, debt issuances and EU-funds flows since 2005.
indicators_governance_description: Corruption perceptions, World Bank governance scores and Eurobarometer trust in institutions.
indicators_society_description: Youth unemployment, house prices, income inequality and at-risk-of-poverty rate.
eu_compare_page_description: Bulgaria side-by-side with EU27 and four neighbour/CEE peers (Romania, Greece, Hungary, Croatia) — governance, budget, income, longevity.
eu_compare_cabinet_anchor_label: Pick a cabinet to see the data at the end of its term
eu_compare_callout_header_finished: Panels below show values at the end of:
eu_compare_callout_header_incumbent: Panels below show the latest available values (cabinet still in office):
eu_compare_callout_no_selection: No cabinet picked — panels below show values at the selected election.
eu_compare_section_wgi_explainer: The World Bank's six criteria for quality of governance: voice & accountability, political stability, government effectiveness, regulatory quality, rule of law, and control of corruption. Countries are scored from −2.5 to +2.5, with 0 the world mean. Bulgaria has consistently trailed the EU average on all six — the chart shows where the gap is widest.
eu_compare_section_snapshot_explainer: The table shows the most recent value for each indicator for Bulgaria and the visible peers, along with Bulgaria's rank in the EU27 where the direction is unambiguous. Cells are coloured against the EU27 average according to each indicator's logic: lower-is-better for inflation, debt and unemployment; higher-is-better for GDP growth and budget balance.
eu_compare_section_snapshot_footnote: Source: Eurostat. Peer values may lag Bulgaria by up to 4 quarters when the peer's series publishes later — hover a cell to see the period offset.
eu_compare_section_cofog_explainer: General-government expenditure broken out by function, as % of GDP. The full bar height shows the share of government spending in the economy — countries with larger budgets stand taller. The coloured segments show what the money goes toward. Below the chart: the largest gaps between Bulgaria and the EU27 average.
eu_compare_cofog_delta_less: Bulgaria spends {{pp}} pp less of GDP on {{function}} than the EU27 average (BG {{bg}}% vs EU {{eu}}%).
eu_compare_cofog_delta_more: Bulgaria spends {{pp}} pp more of GDP on {{function}} than the EU27 average (BG {{bg}}% vs EU {{eu}}%).
eu_compare_cofog_footnote: Source: Eurostat (gov_10a_exp), {{year}}. Greece does not report a COFOG functional breakdown to Eurostat, so it is omitted from this chart.
eu_compare_section_inequality_explainer: Three key SILC indicators (Statistics on Income and Living Conditions) of inequality and poverty: the Gini coefficient (0 = perfect equality, 100 = perfect inequality), the S80/S20 ratio (income share of the top 20% relative to the bottom 20%), and the AROPE index (share of population at risk of poverty or social exclusion). For all three, a lower number is the better result. Unfortunately, Bulgaria ranks last (27/27) in the EU on all three.
eu_compare_section_scatters_explainer: Is the money worth it? Each scatter pairs government spending (% of GDP) against the resulting outcome for the same peer set. Dashed lines mark the EU27 average and split the chart into four zones: top-left (the ideal corner) — high outcomes at low spend, money is being managed efficiently; bottom-right (the problem corner) — low outcomes despite high spend, a sign of inefficiency.
eu_compare_scatter_health_explainer: Government health spend (% of GDP) against life expectancy at birth. Bulgaria allocates a noticeably smaller share of its economy to health than the EU average, and at the same time citizens live meaningfully fewer years. The combination — low spend and low outcome — points to both underfunding and questions about how efficiently the money is used.
eu_compare_scatter_social_title: Social protection: spend vs poverty risk
eu_compare_scatter_social_explainer: Government social-protection spend (% of GDP) against the AROPE index — the share of population at risk of poverty or social exclusion. Bulgaria allocates a meaningfully smaller share of its economy to social protection than the EU27 average (below most peers as well), and at the same time records the EU's highest poverty risk.
eu_compare_sources_eurostat: Eurostat — macro and fiscal indicators (gov_10a_main, gov_10q_ggdebt, namq_10_gdp, prc_hicp_minr, une_rt_q and others). Fetched: {{date}}.
eu_compare_sources_silc: Eurostat SILC — inequality and poverty (ilc_di12, ilc_di11, ilc_peps01n) + demo_mlexpec for life expectancy. Fetched: {{date}}.
eu_compare_sources_cofog: Eurostat gov_10a_exp — functional classification of government expenditure (COFOG). Fetched: {{date}}.
eu_compare_sources_wgi: World Bank — Worldwide Governance Indicators (source 3, GOV_WGI_*.EST). Fetched: {{date}}.
eu_compare_sources_eu27_synth: The EU27 average on the WGI radar is an unweighted mean across the 27 member states — the World Bank does not publish a regional aggregate.
cabinet_score_row_heading: By cabinet — averages over the term
cabinet_selector_hint: Click a pill above to see that cabinet's term-average indicators.
demographics_description: Bulgaria's 2021 census mapped onto our regional electoral data — ethnicity, religion, education and age structure side by side with vote shares.
demographics_explainer: All figures come from the National Statistical Institute's 2021 Population and Housing Census. Use the controls below to compare oblasts, see per-region breakdowns on the map, or correlate any demographic with a party's municipal vote share.
demographics_regions_description: Census 2021 demographics for Bulgaria's 28 administrative oblasts — sortable by every column.
demographics_municipalities_description: Census 2021 demographics for Bulgaria's 265 municipalities — sortable, filterable by name, paginated 30 per page.
census_crosstab_explainer: Each bubble is one municipality. Pick a party and a demographic dimension to see how the two correlate across all 265 municipalities. Sofia city is shown as a single bubble.
census_correlation_positive: {{party}} tends to overperform in municipalities with a higher {{metric}} (r = {{r}}).
census_correlation_negative: {{party}} tends to underperform in municipalities with a higher {{metric}} (r = {{r}}).
census_scatter_note: Vote share by municipality for the {{date}} election; demographics from the 2021 census.
census_tooltip_click_hint: Click to open the municipality page
census_tile_hint: Snapshot from the National Statistical Institute's 2021 Population and Housing Census ({{date}}).
census_settlement_dimensions_note: Ethnicity, religion and education are only published at the municipality level by NSI; settlements show population, age and sex.
regional_tile_hint: Latest annual values per oblast (Eurostat NUTS 3 + Агенция по заетостта) with year-over-year change.
regional_sofia_oblast_footnote: Eurostat publishes Sofia city as one NUTS 3 entity — the same value applies to all three Sofia electoral districts.
indicators_tile_hint: Latest annual values for the municipality with year-over-year change.
indicators_sofia_city_footnote: Sofia city is published as a single value; the same number applies to all 24 city districts.
indicators_map_explainer: Latest annual values per municipality. Click a municipality to drill down. Sofia city is published as one value, so all 24 districts share the same number.
regional_map_explainer: Latest annual values per oblast — Eurostat NUTS 3 indicators plus long-term unemployment from Агенция по заетостта. Click an oblast to drill down. Sofia city (BG411) is published as one entity, so all three Sofia electoral districts share the same value.
eurostat_macro_source: Eurostat — macroeconomic indicators (GDP, HICP, unemployment, fiscal balances, house-price index, FDI flows BPM6); fetched for Bulgaria and for the EU27 aggregate + Romania, Greece, Hungary and Croatia. Surfaced on /indicators as a KPI dashboard + four domain sub-pages (economy, fiscal, governance, society) plus a peer-explorer at /indicators/compare
eurostat_regional_source: Eurostat — NUTS 3 (per-oblast) indicators: GDP per capita, population, net migration
eurostat_cofog_source: Eurostat gov_10a_exp — general-government expenditure by COFOG-99 function: the ten top-level functions (general services, defence, health, education, social protection, etc.) as % of GDP. Powers the functional-classification tile on /budget, the "what did your taxes buy" calculator, and the side-by-side per-country stacked bars on /indicators/compare
eurostat_silc_source: Eurostat SILC — income and living conditions: Gini coefficient (ilc_di12), S80/S20 income quintile share ratio (ilc_di11), AROPE — at-risk-of-poverty-or-social-exclusion rate (ilc_peps01n), overall poverty risk (ilc_li02). Powers the inequality panel + spend-vs-outcome scatters on /indicators/compare
eurostat_life_expectancy_source: Eurostat demo_mlexpec — life expectancy at birth by sex and age. Powers the "health spend vs life expectancy" scatter on /indicators/compare
sovereign_debt_bnb_source: Bulgarian National Bank — Fiscal Agent auction archive (domestic ДЦК, 2019+)
sovereign_debt_luxse_source: Luxembourg Stock Exchange — Republic of Bulgaria Eurobond listings (since 2002)
sovereign_debt_minfin_source: Bulgarian Ministry of Finance — monthly debt bulletin + Eurobond press releases
governance_ti_cpi_source: Transparency International — Corruption Perceptions Index (CPI), Bulgaria
governance_wb_wgi_source: World Bank — Worldwide Governance Indicators (WGI), Bulgaria. /governments shows Rule of Law, Control of Corruption and Government Effectiveness; /indicators/compare draws all six dimensions (plus Voice & Accountability, Political Stability, Regulatory Quality) as a radar for Bulgaria and the peer set. The EU27 mean is an unweighted average computed locally — the World Bank does not publish a regional aggregate
governance_eurobarometer_source: Standard Eurobarometer — "tend to trust" share for parliament, government and the EU (Bulgaria); annual mean of spring + autumn waves. Powers the trust series on /indicators/governance
indicators_az_source: Агенция по заетостта — registered unemployment per municipality (annual review, XLSX)
indicators_mon_dzi_source: Министерство на образованието via data.egov.bg — state-matura (DZI) results per school, aggregated to municipality
indicators_nsi_pop_source: Национален статистически институт — annual population per municipality (Pop_6.1.1 timeseries XLSX)
indicators_nsi_vital_source: Национален статистически институт — annual births, deaths and internal migration per municipality (timeseries XLSX) — feeds natural population change + net migration
indicators_grao_source: ГРАО — settlement-level population by permanent and current address (quarterly t41nm tables)
procurement_aop_source: Агенция за обществени поръчки (АОП) via data.egov.bg — fortnightly OCDS-standard contract bundles (since 2026) and annual CSV dumps for prior years (2011-2023)
procurement_aop_debarred_source: АОП "Стопански субекти с нарушения" register (www2.aop.bg) — debarred-suppliers list, refreshed when КЗК rulings become final. Drives the "debarred" red-flag chip on contract rows.
eu_funds_isun_source: ИСУН 2020 public module (2020.eufunds.bg) — the EU-funds Management & Monitoring Information System. The public beneficiary register lists every organisation that has signed an EU-funds contract (2014-2020, 2021-2027 and the Recovery Plan), with contracts signed, funds contracted and funds actually paid.
funds_index_intro: Every organisation that has signed an EU-funds contract recorded in ИСУН 2020 — the 2014-2020 and 2021-2027 programmes plus the Recovery Plan.
funds_index_beneficiaries_hint: Organisations with at least one EU-funds contract, as listed in the ИСУН register (a parent and its sub-units count separately).
funds_index_contracted_hint: Total value of signed EU-funds contracts (Договорени средства).
funds_index_paid_hint: Total actually disbursed to beneficiaries (Реално изплатени суми).
funds_index_mp_hint: MPs whose declared business interests intersect EU-funds beneficiaries.
funds_index_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 public beneficiary register. “Contracted funds” is the total contracted project value per ИСУН — for some programmes it includes the beneficiary’s own co-financing, not only the EU grant.
funds_mp_intro: Beneficiary companies in which a sitting or former MP has a declared ownership stake or a Commerce Registry management role.
funds_mp_source_hint: MP linkages from Court-of-Audit declarations and Commerce Registry filings. A connection describes what the MP is on record for — not in itself an accusation of wrongdoing.
funds_tile_more_below: Showing top beneficiaries by funds contracted; click “See all” for the full list.
funds_tile_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020. Joined to MP business filings (cacbg + TR).
funds_no_connected_long: No EU-funds beneficiaries were found for companies connected to this candidate. Either the MP's known business graph doesn't intersect the ИСУН register, or no such company has signed an EU-funds contract.
funds_page_intro: Each row is a company that received an EU-funds contract AND has a recorded business linkage (Commerce Registry role or property-declaration stake) to this MP.
funds_page_source_hint: Source: ИСУН 2020 public beneficiary register. MP linkages from cacbg property declarations and Commerce Registry filings. Connections describe what the MP has declared or is on record for — they are not in themselves an accusation of wrongdoing. “Contracted funds” is the total contracted project value per ИСУН — for some programmes it includes the beneficiary’s own co-financing, not only the EU grant.
budget_kfp_source: Министерство на финансите via data.egov.bg — Консолидирана фискална програма (КФП), monthly state-budget execution snapshots
budget_law_dv_source: Държавен вестник — annual Закон за държавния бюджет (HTML), per-spending-unit appropriations at admin + program grain
budget_ministry_execution_source: Per-ministry annual "Отчет за изпълнението на програмния бюджет" (PDF / XLSX-in-ZIP / DOCX / DOCX-in-ZIP), published by each first-level spending unit. Cloudflare-blocked sites (МФ, МВР) use a manual-pdf cache path — МФ FY2023 was backfilled from the Internet Archive. JS-rendered ministry sites (МВнР, МК, МОН, МРРБ, МЕ, МС, МТС) are surveyed via a Playwright discovery tool plus Wayback-CDX watchers; МВнР FY2023 is ingested from its ZIP-packaged XLSX. Personnel-data coverage: ~30% of total first-level FY2024 expenditure plus МФ and МВнР for FY2023; МО redacts staffing as classified; the rest of the gap is ministries that don't publish their programme-budget execution report at a stable URL
budget_ec_eu_funds_source: European Commission — "EU spending and revenue" per-Member-State XLSX (Bulgaria totals of EU expenditure and national contribution)
budget_cofog_source: Eurostat gov_10a_exp — general-government expenditure by COFOG-99 function (sector S13, annual). Covers state budget plus social-security funds (NOI / NHIF), municipal budgets and EU funds — broader than the state-budget execution feed.
budget_eu_peers_source: Eurostat gov_10a_main — general-government revenue, expenditure and balance as % of GDP (sector S13, annual). Powers the EU peer-comparison chart on /governance and the BG-vs-EU27 rank chips on the /budget headline cards (Bulgaria's position within the 27 member states + EU27 average).
budget_customs_revenue_source: Агенция "Митници" — "Митническа хроника" annual report (PDF). Itemises excise revenue by product group (fuels → diesel/petrol/LPG/natural-gas/kerosene + tobacco + alcohol), import VAT total + by BEC commercial category, and customs duties total + top-5 country of origin. Hand-curated URLs per fiscal year; output under data/budget/revenue_breakdown/customs/. Reconciles to KFP excise + customs duties totals exactly across 2022-2025.
budget_nap_annual_source: НАП — "Годишен отчет за дейността на Националната агенция за приходите" (PDF, approved by Council of Ministers in March of T+1). Carries declared net VAT by 21 КИД-2008 economic sectors (Table 3), PIT receipts by income type — employment / freelance / final tax + dividends — and Table 9 employment-PIT due contributions by sector (Jan-Nov coverage). Drives data/budget/revenue_breakdown/vat/ and data/budget/revenue_breakdown/pit/.
budget_iisda_doklad_source: ИИСДА — "Доклад за състоянието на администрацията", an annual report on the state of the Bulgarian administration published by the Council of Ministers (PDF). Carries total positions (щатна численост) and filled/vacant counts across central + territorial administration, headcount by administration type (Министерства, Държавни агенции, Изпълнителни агенции, Областни администрации, Общински администрации, …), and NSI list-headcount (excludes МВР + МО). Covers 2017-2025. Combined with per-programme "Численост на щатния персонал" rows extracted from each ministry's program-budget execution report, it powers the Personnel tile on /budget, the Sankey drill-down, and the per-programme headcount + average annual cost per FTE on each ministry detail page.
fiscal_reserve_minfin_xlsx_source: Министерство на финансите — Фискален резерв (monthly FRA-MM-YYYY-BG.xlsx). Authoritative end-of-month stock; unit switched from BGN to EUR with the 2026-01-01 euro adoption. Fetched via Wayback Machine where cached, manual drop otherwise.
fiscal_reserve_minfin_mreport_source: Министерство на финансите — Месечен бюлетин (mreport PDF). КФП summary table carrying the фискален резерв row across ~12 rolling months. Secondary cross-validation source.
fiscal_reserve_minfin_buletin_source: Министерство на финансите — Информационен бюлетин (BULETIN PDF). Narrative press-bulletin with the single end-of-month figure stated inline. Third cross-validation source.
dashboard_benford_hint: Number of parties whose first- or second-digit distribution of section vote counts deviates from Benford's expectation (MAD). This is a screening signal, not evidence of fraud.
dashboard_risk_score_hint: Number of sections in the Critical band on a composite score from seven statistical signals. A high score means the section warrants a closer look — not a verdict.
dashboard_risk_score_critical_suffix: sections in Critical band (of {{total}} total sections, {{pct}})
risk_analysis_description: A consolidated view of every statistical screening signal we publish for this election: section-level risk score, Benford digit distributions, voting-machine flash-memory drift, vote concentration, and at-risk neighborhoods. Each section is a summary that links to the full data.
risk_analysis_caveat_title: This is a screening tool, not a verdict
risk_analysis_caveat_body: This page combines several screening signals. Each has innocent explanations (small section size, demographic homogeneity, late registration drives, lawful recount). Always inspect the breakdown for the specific section or party before drawing conclusions.
risk_clusters_hint: Knots of physically adjacent polling sections that all screen elevated-or-above and were won by the same party — the geographic fingerprint of a controlled or corporate vote. A view over the risk scores, not a verdict.
risk_clusters_headline: {{count}} clusters of adjacent same-party sections group {{sectioned}} polling sections. {{total}} sections in total screen elevated or above — use the toggles to filter the map.
risk_clusters_headline_none: No section clusters detected. {{total}} sections screen elevated or above — use the band toggles to filter the map.
risk_history_hint: Every election this polling section took part in, side by side — winner, turnout and the risk SCREENING score. A view over published data, not a verdict.
risk_history_headline: Screened elevated or above in {{count}} of {{total}} elections.
risk_history_headline_none: No elevated screening risk in any of {{total}} elections.
risk_persistence_hint: Geographic knots of adjacent same-party sections that clustered — screened elevated or above as a bloc — in two or more elections. A knot that recurs cycle after cycle is the fingerprint of a standing vote-control operation, not a one-off. A view over the risk clusters, not a verdict.
risk_persistence_headline: {{count}} geographic knots clustered in two or more elections; {{deep}} recurred in three or more. Each dot is one election, coloured by the winning party.
risk_persistence_not_found: This cluster could not be found.
risk_persistence_detail_summary: Clustered in {{count}} elections — {{sections}} polling sections in total.
risk_persistence_sections_hint: Every polling section that belonged to the cluster in at least one election. Open one for its full cross-election risk history.
risk_persistence_problem_aggregate: {{count}} also coincide with a Roma neighbourhood already flagged for controlled-vote risk.
risk_persistence_problem_overlap: {{count}} of {{total}} sections lie in {{name}} — a flagged problem neighbourhood.
risk_analysis_sections_hint: Composite 0–100 score from seven statistical signals: flash-memory drift, recount corrections, invalid ballots, additional voters, vote concentration, peer-section outliers, shift vs. the previous election.
risk_analysis_sections_headline: {{critical}} sections fall in the Critical band ({{criticalPct}}); {{highOrAbove}} are High or Critical ({{highOrAbovePct}}).
risk_analysis_benford_hint: Parties whose section-vote digit distribution deviates from the Benford expectation. Election-forensics literature (Mebane) recommends the second-digit test (2BL) over the first-digit test for vote counts.
risk_analysis_benford_headline: {{strong}} parties with strong deviation (MAD ≥ 0.08), {{moderate}} more with moderate (0.04–0.08). {{total}} parties tested.
risk_analysis_benford_none: None of the {{total}} tested parties show significant deviation from Benford.
risk_analysis_link_risk_score_desc: 0–100 composite score per polling section, with a breakdown across the seven signals.
risk_analysis_link_benford_desc: First- and second-digit distribution of section vote counts, per party.
risk_analysis_link_flash_desc: Per-party comparison of votes recorded on the machine flash vs. on the protocols.
risk_analysis_link_concentrated_desc: Settlements where a single party receives over 80% of the valid vote.
risk_analysis_link_problem_sections_desc: Polling sections in Roma neighborhoods — votes, turnout, leading party.
risk_analysis_link_additional_voters_des: Sections with unusually many voters added to the electoral list on election day.
composite_index_hint: 0–100 composite score from five process-integrity signals (section screening, machine integrity, missing flash, concentration, procedural). Equally weighted, averaged only across components that have data. Context signals (Benford, neighborhood swing, electoral volatility, polling error) are shown alongside but do NOT feed the headline — they can light up in perfectly clean elections.
composite_index_caption: Average across {{available}} of {{total}} process-integrity components. Click any component for detail.
composite_context_hint: These signals are informational — they measure statistical fingerprints, demographic volatility, or forecast failure rather than direct irregularity. They are shown separately and do NOT feed into the headline average.
composite_component_sections_hint: Weighted share of national turnout sitting in sections with Elevated/High/Critical screening score (Critical weighted 1.0, High 0.5, Elevated 0.2). Calibrated cap — 5% of turnout = 100.
composite_component_machine_hint: Sum of absolute differences between flash-memory and protocol votes, divided by total machine votes. Calibrated cap — 0.2% drift = 100. The threshold matches international audit-trigger neighborhoods (US recount rules, Mexico INE).
composite_component_concentration_hint: Share of national turnout that went to the top party in settlements where it took ≥80% of valid votes. Calibrated cap — 2% of turnout = 100.
composite_component_procedural_hint: Combined share of national turnout from invalid ballots and additional voters in settlements where those rates exceed 10%. Calibrated cap — 2% of turnout = 100.
composite_component_benford_hint: Share of tested parties (with at least 100 sections) that show strong deviation from Benford (MAD ≥ 0.08) on the second-digit test. Mebane's recommended test for vote-count data.
composite_component_neighborhoodsSwing_h: Change in the top party's vote share inside the eight tracked communities versus the prior cycle, MINUS that party's national change over the same pair of cycles. A much cleaner signal than the static demographic share — it isolates the cycle-specific movement that the vote-buying literature (Stokes et al. 2013; Cantú 2014) associates with broker-managed turnout. Calibrated cap: 15 pp excess swing = 100.
composite_component_voteSwitching_hint: Pedersen (1979) index: 0.5 × Σ|Δshare| across parties between this cycle and the prior. The standard comparative-politics measure of net vote transfer between parties. Below 5 (stable democracies) = 0; above 30 (hyper-volatile cycles like BG 2021) = 100. High values do NOT imply irregularity — usually they signal a new entrant or major realignment.
composite_component_missingFlash_hint: Share of total machine votes sitting in sections that ran machine voting but submitted no flash drive at protocol time. These votes are not end-to-end auditable. Calibrated cap — 1% of machine votes = 100.
composite_component_polls_hint: Pollster mean absolute error (MAE) averaged across all agencies for this election. Below 1.5 pp (international baseline) = 0; above 5 pp = 100. CONTEXT signal, not integrity — high values mean forecast failure (methodology, late deciders, legal blackout window, new entrant), not election irregularity. This is why it is excluded from the headline index.
composite_component_clusters_hint: Share of the geolocatable elevated-or-above sections that fall into a same-party geographic cluster — a knot of adjacent flagged sections, the controlled/corporate-vote fingerprint. Below 5% (irreducible structural clustering) = 0; above 20% = 100. CONTEXT signal — it re-describes sections already counted by the section-screening integrity component, and clustering is partly structural, so it never feeds the headline.
risk_analysis_polls_disclaimer_short: Polling error does not imply election irregularity.
risk_analysis_polls_hint: Comparison of pollster mean absolute error for this election against the historical norm. This is a context signal — not part of the composite risk index. Polling error has structural causes (methodology, late deciders, sample representativeness) that would fire this signal even in clean elections.
risk_analysis_polls_headline_with_baseli: {{count}} agencies, mean error {{mean}} percentage points (historical mean for BG: {{baseline}}).
risk_analysis_polls_disclaimer: Context signal, not part of the composite index. Polling error does not imply election irregularity.
composite_methodology_description: How the composite index is computed — the five process-integrity signals that form the headline score, and the four context signals shown alongside.
composite_methodology_caveat_title: An analysis tool, not a verdict
composite_methodology_caveat_body: A high index value means an election is statistically unusual along several independent measures and deserves closer examination — nothing more. Each component can have a perfectly logical explanation, so always inspect the detailed breakdown before drawing conclusions.
composite_methodology_intro: The Election Risk Index is a 0–100 score that summarises five independent process-integrity signals. It sits at the top of the /risk-analysis page with the full breakdown, and in compact form on the home page under the Anomalies section. Alongside it we show four context signals (Benford, neighborhood swing, electoral volatility, polling error) that do NOT feed the headline — they can light up in perfectly clean elections.
composite_methodology_p_what1: The headline is the arithmetic mean of five process-integrity signals: section screening, machine integrity, missing flash, concentration, and procedural anomalies. Each is vote-weighted (as a share of national turnout, or of total machine votes for the machine-only signals) so it reads as "what fraction of the result this signal touches," not "how many places are flagged."
composite_methodology_p_what2: The formula applies only to available data. If older elections lack data (e.g. flash memory predates 2021), the index is computed from the remaining available process-integrity signals. Context signals are shown for every cycle where their data is available.
composite_methodology_p_what3: Splitting the signals into two tracks follows the international electoral-integrity frameworks (Pippa Norris PEI, V-Dem Election Quality Index, IFES). They do not mix process signals (audit-trail loss, protocol/flash disagreement) with context signals (statistical fingerprints, demographic volatility, forecast failure). Polling error in particular is NOT an irregularity signal — it is a prompt to inspect the process-integrity components more carefully.
composite_methodology_p_components_intro: Each component has a value from 0 to 100, and the links in the UI lead to the full source data for each. The headline is their simple average.
composite_methodology_c_sections_p: weighted share of national turnout sitting in sections with Elevated/High/Critical screening score (Critical weighted 1.0, High 0.5, Elevated 0.2). Calibrated cap — 5% of turnout = 100.
composite_methodology_c_machine_p: sum of absolute differences between flash memory and protocol per party, scaled to total machine votes. Calibrated cap — 0.2% drift = 100 (matching international audit-trigger thresholds).
composite_methodology_c_missingFlash_p: share of total machine votes sitting in sections with machine voting but missing flash data. These votes cannot be fully audited. (Cap: 1% of machine votes).
composite_methodology_c_concentration_p: share of national turnout that went to the top party in settlements where it took ≥80% of valid votes. The classic signal of a controlled or 'captured' electorate. (Cap: 2% of turnout).
composite_methodology_c_procedural_p: combined share of national turnout from invalid ballots and additional voters in settlements where those rates exceed 10%. These two factors are often signs of organised or broker-driven voting. (Cap: 2% of turnout).
composite_methodology_p_context_intro: These signals are shown in a separate row and do NOT feed the headline average. They commonly light up in perfectly clean elections (new entrants, late deciders, range-bounded vote counts that fail Benford by construction).
composite_methodology_c_benford_p: share of parties (with over 100 sections) that show strong deviation (MAD ≥ 0.08) on the second-digit test. Benford on vote counts often fails by construction (Mebane 2006), which is why it is a context signal.
composite_methodology_c_neighborhoodsSwi: change in the top party's vote share in the eight tracked communities versus the prior cycle, minus the same party's national change. Isolates cycle-specific movement that the vote-buying literature (Stokes et al. 2013; Cantú 2014) associates with broker-managed turnout. Cap: 15 pp excess swing = 100.
composite_methodology_c_voteSwitching_p: Pedersen (1979) index: 0.5 × Σ|Δshare| across parties between this cycle and the prior. Below 5 (stable democracies) = 0; above 30 (hyper-volatile cycles) = 100. High values do NOT imply irregularity — usually a new entrant or major realignment.
composite_methodology_c_polls_p: mean absolute error (MAE) of agencies for the cycle. A high value here signals forecast failure (new entrant, late deciders, legal blackout window), not an election irregularity. Excluded from the headline for that reason — but it should prompt a closer look at the process-integrity signals.
composite_methodology_c_clusters_p: share of the geolocatable elevated-risk sections that sit in a same-party geographic cluster — a knot of adjacent flagged sections won by one party. Calibrated on the 2005–2026 backtest (a stable 8–17% band): below 5% = 0, above 20% = 100. A context signal — it re-describes sections the section-screening component already counts, and clustering is partly a structural artefact of geographically correlated risk factors, so it is kept out of the headline.
composite_methodology_p_bands_intro: Bands are intentionally asymmetric, since the actual distribution in Bulgaria (2009–2026) shows that most 'clean' cycles fall in the 0–25 range.
composite_methodology_band_calm: No unusual signals on any component.
composite_methodology_band_elevated: At least one component shows mid-level deviation; typical for most post-2021 elections.
composite_methodology_band_high: Several components are simultaneously above mid-level; warrants careful analysis.
composite_methodology_band_critical: Many components are near their cap; extremely rare historically.
composite_methodology_important_caps_p: Component thresholds are calibrated to actually-observed data in 2009–2026, not to theoretical maxima.
composite_methodology_important_availabl: The headline uses all five process-integrity signals for elections from July 2021 onwards. For older cycles (before mass machine voting was introduced), 'Machine integrity' and 'Missing flash memory' are skipped and the index is computed from the remaining three. The comparison is fair but based on less information. Context signals are available only when their data exists for the cycle (neighborhood swing and electoral volatility require a prior cycle for comparison).
composite_methodology_important_polls_t: Why context signals are excluded from the headline:
composite_methodology_important_polls_p: Benford on vote counts often fails by construction (the test requires data spanning several orders of magnitude, but per-section party vote counts cluster in a narrow range). Polling error has structural causes unrelated to the electoral process (legal blackout, new entrants, late deciders). Electoral volatility measures political instability, not irregularity. Neighborhood swing is a cycle-on-cycle signal — substantive but threshold-sensitive. All four are shown, but outside the headline average.
composite_methodology_important_evidence: The index is a starting point for investigation, not a final piece of evidence. Always analyse which specific components form the final score.

personnel_ministries_caveat: Coverage: ~30% of total first-level FY2024 expenditure (€2.7B of €8.9B). Includes only ministries whose programme-budget execution report has been ingested. MoD redacts the staffing numbers (classified); MoI and MoF FY2024 are Cloudflare-challenged (MoF FY2023 was backfilled via the Internet Archive). The remaining ~70% — including Interior, Education, Regional Development, Transport, Justice, Culture, Foreign Affairs — don't publish their programme-budget execution report at a stable URL.

budget_tax_bill_above_cap_hint: Above the МОД cap each extra euro is taxed at only 10% — the effective rate falls as the salary rises.
budget_tax_bill_include_employer: Include employer contributions in the breakdown below
budget_tax_bill_vat_note: 20% VAT embedded in everyday spending, assuming {{pct}} of net income goes to standard-rated goods.
budget_tax_bill_pension_into: Paid into the pension system: {{total}}{{per}} (you {{you}} + employer {{employer}}).
budget_tax_bill_pension_into_self: Paid into the pension system: {{total}}{{per}} (you remit the full amount).
budget_tax_bill_pension_ages: Retirement in 2026: women at 62y 6m (36y 10m of service), men at 64y 9m (39y 10m of service).
budget_tax_bill_pension_caption: Simplified: pension ≈ insurable income × 1.35% × years of service, bounded by the 2026 minimum (€322) and maximum (€1,738) pension. The real formula weighs lifetime earnings against the national average.
budget_tax_bill_caption: Approximate estimate. Assumes a default-case taxpayer (born after 1959, no second-pillar opt-out). SSC stops accruing above the МОД; above the cap only the 10% income tax keeps scaling. Social-security contributions are earmarked (pension + health + unemployment), not freely allocable — the breakdown applies general-government spending shares to the whole bill purely for illustration.
budget_tax_bill_company_caption: Simplified: 10% corporate tax on profit, then 5% tax when the remainder is distributed as a dividend. Corporate tax is assessed annually; the owner's own social-security contributions are not modelled here.
budget_tax_bill_curve_title: Effective rate across the salary range
budget_tax_bill_curve_caption: Effective tax-and-contribution rate by gross salary. Once salary passes the МОД cap social security stops growing and the rate falls — the burden turns regressive at the top.
budget_tax_bill_tip_ssc: Employee social-security and health contributions — pension 8.78%, health 3.20%, sickness/maternity 1.40%, unemployment 0.40%. Charged on insurable income up to the МОД cap.
budget_tax_bill_tip_mod: Maximum insurable income (МОД) — the monthly ceiling above which no further social-security contributions are due. Income tax still applies to the whole salary.
budget_tax_bill_tip_rate: Effective rate = total tax and contributions ÷ gross income. Marginal rate = the tax on the next euro earned; it falls to 10% above the МОД cap, where social security stops.
budget_tax_bill_tip_wedge: Tax wedge — the share of the full cost of employing you (gross salary plus employer contributions) taken by tax and social security rather than reaching your take-home pay.

budget_journey_subtitle: Law → execution → audit, per fiscal year.
budget_methodology_description: Where the budget data comes from, how it is processed, and what the dashboard covers.
budget_meth_source_p: The figures come from the Ministry of Finance dataset “state budget execution by major budget indicators”, published on the national open-data portal data.egov.bg under a public-domain (CC0) licence. The Ministry publishes one resource per monthly cash-execution snapshot; each lists, for the five top-level sections of the state budget, the amount set by the budget law and the amount executed so far that fiscal year.
budget_meth_sections_p: The Bulgarian state budget execution table has five top-level sections, and the dashboard tracks each of them over time:
budget_meth_revenue: — tax and non-tax revenue, grants and donations.
budget_meth_expenditure: — spending and transfers: personnel, operations, capital, social spending, subsidies, interest.
budget_meth_eu: — Bulgaria's contribution to the common EU budget.
budget_meth_balance: — revenue minus expenditure minus the EU contribution. Negative means a deficit.
budget_meth_financing: — how the balance is financed: external and domestic borrowing, deposits, privatisation.
budget_meth_currency_p: Bulgaria adopted the euro on 1 January 2026. Monthly snapshots up to and including December 2025 are published in leva; from January 2026 they are in euro. Every figure on the dashboard is shown in euro.
budget_meth_cumulative_p: Each monthly snapshot is cumulative year-to-date: the December figure is the whole year, and the series resets each January. The execution-trend chart shows this directly — a ramp within each fiscal year, then a reset.
budget_meth_integrity_p: The ingest fails loudly rather than publish partial data: a pinned snapshot is re-parsed on every run and byte-compared against a committed fixture, so any drift in the parser is caught before anything is written, and a change touching more than a small fraction of the data aborts the run for review.
budget_meth_ministries_p: Each first-level spending unit (ministry, agency, independent body) publishes its own annual execution report. The ingest parses these PDFs into a uniform admin-grain table and reconciles them against the State Budget Law, so each ministry page shows planned versus actual at the ministry level and one column deeper at the program level.
budget_meth_documents_p: Each fiscal year is linked to its source documents — the State Budget Law as promulgated in Държавен вестник, the mid-year amendment laws, the year-end execution report, and the report of the National Audit Office. The fiscal-year page surfaces the timeline and the original links.
budget_meth_procurement_p: Each spending unit's page links to the public-procurement contracts it awarded under the Public Procurement Act, so a budget line can be followed all the way down to the individual contracts that consumed it.

chart_event_gfc_desc: Contraction of world trade and capital flows after the Lehman Brothers collapse (Sep 2008). For Bulgaria — sharp GDP fall and unemployment spike through 2009–2010.
chart_event_borisov1_protests_desc: Anti-monopoly and electricity-bill protests in Feb–Mar 2013 that brought down GERB's first cabinet four months before the regular election.
chart_event_dans_desc: Year-long civic mobilisation against the Oresharski cabinet, started days after Delyan Peevski was appointed head of DANS (June 2013).
chart_event_ktb_desc: Bank run and licence withdrawal of Corporate Commercial Bank in summer 2014 — Bulgaria's largest banking crisis since the 1990s. Hit the fiscal reserve and trust in the banking supervisor.
chart_event_covid_desc: Global pandemic from the WHO declaration (March 2020) to the lifting of Bulgaria's epidemic state of emergency (April 2022). Drove the 2020 GDP contraction and the 2021–2023 inflation cycle.
chart_event_borisov3_protests_desc: Anti-government and anti-corruption protests through summer and autumn 2020 — did not topple the cabinet but reshaped the 2021 election cycle.
chart_event_autumn_2025_protests_desc: Civic protests in autumn 2025, during the first year of the Zhelyazkov cabinet's term.
